title: Defect Reduction Engineer
description: **Job Details** Job Description: Defect Reduction Engineers are responsible for the development and sustaining of integrated defect monitors in Intel's semiconductor processes. Integrated defect monitors are a key component of delivering world class yields for our customers. **Responsibilities** As part of your role, you will work in the Defect Metrology area as part of a team of layer owners. In this role, key responsibilities include: * Own multiple integrated monitors. * Develop solutions to problems utilizing statistical knowledge and problem\-solving tools as well as developing strategies to prevent the recurrence of the problem in future. * Troubleshooting complex issues, developing scalable solutions, and implementing strategies to prevent recurrence. * Continuously improve tool performance and optimize recipes through sustaining and innovation activities. * Lead continuous improvement initiatives with your team organization which will improve the operational performance of the area. **Qualifications** **Minimum Requirements** * Level 8 (NFQ) degree or higher in Science or Engineering (e.g., Physics, Chemistry, Materials Science, Electrical/Mechanical/Chemical Engineering, Mechatronics, Biomedical Engineering, Nanotechnology, Mathematics, or related field) * 4\+ years of experience in an engineering environment * Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills with ability to work independently and take ownership * Proven data analysis skills, including statistical analysis and visualization (e.g., JMP or similar tools) * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work effectively in cross‑functional and multicultural teams **Preferred Requirements** * Experience in semiconductor, pharmaceutical, biomedical, medical device, or advanced manufacturing environments * High attention to detail and strong adherence to procedures * Good planning and organizational skills Requirements listed would be obtained through a combination of industry relevant job experience / research. Knowledge of processes such as diffusion, etching, plating, lithography, and thin film deposition in semiconductor fab manufacturing operations would be an advantage. **Job Type** Experienced Hire **Shift** Shift 1 (Ireland) Primary Location: Ireland, Leixlip Additional Locations: **Business Group** Intel Foundry strives to make every facet of semiconductor manufacturing state\-of\-the\-art while delighting our customers \-\- from delivering cutting\-edge silicon process and packaging technology leadership for the AI era, enabling our customers to design leadership products, global manufacturing scale and supply chain, through the continuous yield improvements to advanced packaging all the way to final test and assembly. We ensure our foundry customers' products receive our utmost focus in terms of service, technology enablement and capacity commitments. Employees in the Foundry Technology Manufacturing are part of a worldwide factory network that designs, develops, manufactures, and assembly/test packages the compute devices to improve the lives of every person on Earth. **Posting Statement** All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. Position of Trust N/A EU Salary We offer a total compensation package that ranks among the best in the industry. Annual Salary Range for this job which could be performed in Ireland is: ‏59,200\.00 € \- ‏111,300\.00 € The compensation range displayed in this posting reflects the minimum and maximum target compensation for this position. Within this range, individual pay is determined by job related skills, experience, and relevant education, training. As part of our total compensation approach, the final offer may include additional pay components and benefits. Your recruiting team will share the confirmed base salary along with any applicable total compensation elements upon employment offer delivery. **Work Model for this Role** This role will require an on\-site presence. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

title: Deputy Treasurer
description: **Description** Ryanair are recruting for a **Deputy Treasurer** to join Europe’s Largest Airline Group. This is an excellent time to join as we look to grow to over 800 aircraft and 300m guests within the next 8 years. Based In Our Global Head Office In Dublin, You Will Be Report To The Treasurer And Work As Part Of a Key Team With Responsibility For * Cash management \& investment of the group’s funds * Financing the airline’s ongoing aircraft delivery programme (via debt capital markets, structured \& bank finance, sale \& leaseback, operating leases, etc.) * Financial risk management – including market risk (foreign exchange, commodity \& interest rate risk hedging), credit risk \& liquidity risk * Card acquiring \& other payment mechanisms * Analysing, enhancing, designing \& implementing treasury systems \& processes * Ensuring compliance with treasury policies \& procedures * Managing the group’s credit lines with financial counterparties * Relationship management (banks, debt investors, lessors, card acquirers \& rating agencies) * Management \& financial reporting (incl. IFRS9 and IFRS16\) * Ad\-hoc projects and duties **Requirements** * A qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) and/or professionally qualified Corporate Treasurer with 3\+ years’ relevant, post qualification, treasury experience. * Excellent analytical and quantitative skills with a keen attention for detail are essential. The candidate should be able to demonstrate a high degree of initiative with strong interpersonal and excellent communication skills. * Experience with a recognised Treasury Management System, Microsoft Excel, Word and PowerPoint is essential. **Benefits** * Highly competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 230 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies * Negotiation Skills * Meeting Deadlines * Results Focus * Leadership

title: Designated Person for Regulatory Compliance (PCF-39F)
description: **Who are we looking for?** We are currently recruiting for Designated Person for Regulatory Compliance (PCF 39\-F) to join our growing Compliance team in our Dublin office. The ideal candidate will be an ambitious, driven compliance professional who is looking to grow a compliance team in a fast\-growing financial services firm. You will work closely with other members of the Senior Management team in Ireland and compliance teams across Carne’s global locations. **What are the minimum requirements you need for the role?** * Minimum of 8\-10 years’ funds industry experience. * University degree (or equivalent qualification) and ideally with the relevant Compliance or legal qualification. * Sound grasp of local legal and regulatory funds framework. * Experience of AIF and UCITS products. * Ideally UCITS Management Company/AIFM and/or MiFID experience but not essential. * Excellent written and oral communication skills. * Ability to analyse complex issues. * Strong ability to evaluate regulations and translate the operational impact. * Experience in carrying out a Compliance Monitoring Programme; and * Team player with a proactive attitude towards anticipating emerging risks. **What is your role and your responsibilities?** * Responsible for analysis and consideration on regulatory matters impacting Carne Irish management company activities as a UCITS management company and AIFM. * Responsible for the liaison with CBI, other local and European authorities and regulators. * Analyse the regulatory rules affecting the Management Company/AIFM business and any changes thereto. * Prepare relevant management information for presentation to the stakeholders and upon request to clients. * Consistently review the compliance policies and procedures to comply with relevant regulations. * Collaborate with the Onboarding, Legal and Oversight teams to provide oversight of third parties. * Support the development of a robust Compliance culture. * Support the further development and oversight of the Compliance Monitoring Programme. * Represent the Compliance function in local or group wide projects and governance committee. * Active participation in the execution of Carne’s compliance monitoring programme. * To support actions relating to training, policies and procedures and due diligence visits; and * To support Carne’s regulatory reporting requirements to the Central Bank of Ireland are met. * Maintain UCITS existing rule mapping in accordance with S.I. 352 of 2011 UCITS Regulations 2011 and S.I. 352 of 2011 that has been agreed for DP Regulatory Compliance and all other DPs for reference based on the CP86/Fund Management Company Guidance. * Quarterly reporting to the board of the Management Company. * Review of valuation errors and investment breaches to the board (errors, breaches, complaints); * Ongoing monitoring of conditions for authorisation including annual update of the Business Plan. * Oversight of Maintenance of Designated email address. * Review record keeping for Management Company records. * Monitoring of Compliance Policies to ensure are up to date review and reporting to the board on same. * Monitor the effectiveness of controls by the Company with applicable anti\-money laundering legislation in conjunction with the MLRO. * Review any proposed related party transactions. **What Competencies do you require?** **Effective Communication:** Communicates effectively, both in an advisory capacity and providing challenge where required **Leading \& Influencing:** Motivate and support others to deliver results, coaching them to optimise performance. Help mentor and develop junior members of the team. **Teamwork (Team player):** Develops relationships within and across teams, working collaboratively to resolve potential challenges quickly. **Drive and Motivation:** Highly motivated and driven, able to focus on what needs to be achieved and enthuses others to do this. **Resilience:** Work well under pressure, bounce back quickly from setbacks in a positive and constructive manner **Client\-centric focus:** Motivated by customer\-facing environments, understanding their needs, and providing a good service. **Planning and Organising:** Organises and plans work effectively, ensuring delivery to quality and timescales in the short and longer\-term **Analytical (Attention to detail):** Analyses and understands relevant and complex information and data to draw appropriate insights. **Strategic Focus (Problem\-Solving):** Thinks strategically, thrives on innovation, considers the wider picture to identify new approaches to enhance business impact **Adaptability:** Adapts approach and responds effectively to different situations, people and new opportunities **Who is Carne Group?** Carne Group has established itself in the funds industry with $2tn in AUM supported by 600\+ staff and four regulated management companies. Together with a large team of industry specialists, Carne serves more than 650\+ traditional and alternative asset management clients from key fund jurisdictions and financial hubs including Ireland, Luxembourg, Zurich, Lisbon, London, New York, Cayman and Channel Islands. Excellence and innovation are central to Carne’s work and we are committed to attracting and retaining the best talent to deliver an unrivalled service to our clients. Whilst working with Carne, you will be provided with opportunities to develop your skills and experience by working in a dynamic, innovative, and multicultural environment where exciting careers are built. We love hearing from anyone who is enthusiastic about changing the asset management industry. Not sure if you meet all the requirements? Let us decide! Research shows that members of other under\-represented groups tend not apply to jobs when they think they may not meet every qualification, when in fact, they do! At Carne we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment and strongly encourage you to apply.

title: Desk Clerk (Transport) - Newbridge
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. Keeping over 220 Lidl stores fully stocked is no small task. That’s the job of our regional distribution centre, matching supply and demand 24/7\. With goods of all kinds flowing in and out, including fresh and frozen produce as well as non\-food, it’s essential that they are received, stored and delivered on time and in excellent condition. Our Regional Distribution Centre is looking for a pro\-active and enthusiastic individual to join their Warehouse team. This role entails various admin tasks, working within both an office and warehouse environment. Reporting to the Deputy Logistics Manager, you will be responsible for ensuring stock is checked, stored and picked efficiently and effectively to be shipped to all stores across the region. You will provide the vital link between the stores and the warehouse ensuring that orders and received and processed in a timely manner. This opportunity is ideal for an individual looking to utilise their strong organisational and interpersonal skills in a buzzing, dynamic team environment. **What You'll Do** * Support the Deputy Logistics Manager with daily tasks, including database management, stock management, the receiving schedule and other relevant computer programmes * Assist the Deputy Logistics Manager with daily operations, including database management, stock control, receiving schedules, and relevant software systems. * Communicate effectively via phone and email with internal departments, stores, and external partners to resolve queries and address any operational issues. * Coordinate with drivers to assign delivery loads and manage the dispatch of goods * Ensure the timely and accurate distribution of goods by coordinating truck dispatches in alignment with delivery schedules. * Monitor and enforce cold chain procedures to preserve product quality for all temperature\-sensitive goods, ensuring compliance with relevant safety and operational standards. * Liaise with GO Checkers to confirm that all incoming goods are accurately Loaded and recorded. * Process and manage all relevant documentation, ensuring accurate data entry into the warehouse management system. * Communicate with drivers to assign bays and coordinate the loading of goods * Assisting the Deputy Logistics Manager and Logistics Manager with any ad hoc tasks **What You'll Need** * Excellent collaboration skills along with the ability to work independently * Proficient in the use of all Microsoft Office programmes * Strong administrative and organisation skills * Confident telephone manner and excellent communication skills * A flexible can\-do attitude * An eye for detail * Outstanding numeracy skills * A sense of pride in a job well done * Experience within a warehouse environment preferable **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €37,500 rising to €51,000 after 3 years * Premium of €25 per shift for unsocial hours * 20 days holiday per annum rising to 25 days after 2 years * Company pension * Private employee medical insurance * Initial training and on\-going development from an experienced team member * Individuals recognised as high performing will be invited to join our Leadership Academy as part of your on\-going development * Circle K discounts available for all employees * Marriage Leave, Employee Assistance Programme * Bike to Work Scheme * On\-site parking * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.

title: Devops Engineer
description: We are seeking a motivated and experienced DevOps Engineer who will be responsible for bridging the gap between development and operations, implementing automation, managing cloud infrastructure, and ensuring smooth deployment pipelines. This role focuses on improving development efficiency, system reliability, and maintaining scalable infrastructure. Good communication skills, motivation and the ability to work within a diverse team dispersed across multiple time\-zones are a must. You will work closely with the Science, Product and Design teams to influence the software development tools, and help drive progress towards the company’s vision of being the leading software developer tool for quantum programming. **Responsibilities** * Infrastructure Management: + Design and maintain cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP) + Implement Infrastructure as Code (IaC) practices + Manage and optimize container orchestration platforms (Kubernetes, Docker Compose) + Configure and maintain monitoring and logging systems + Ensure high availability and disaster recovery solutions * CI/CD Pipeline: + Design and implement continuous integration/continuous deployment pipelines + Automate build, test, and deployment processes + Maintain and improve deployment strategies + Implement security best practices in the pipeline + Monitor and optimize pipeline performance * Automation: + Develop and maintain automation scripts + Implement configuration management tools + Create and maintain infrastructure templates + Automate routine operational tasks + Design self\-healing systems * Security \& Compliance + Implement security best practices + Manage access control and authentication systems + Ensure compliance with security standards + Conduct security audits + Implement vulnerability scanning and remediation * Monitoring \& Performance: + Set up monitoring and alerting systems + Implement logging solutions + Perform system performance optimization + Conduct capacity planning + Troubleshoot production issues * Collaboration: + Work closely with development teams + Provide technical guidance and support + Participate in on\-call rotation + Document processes and procedures + Conduct knowledge sharing sessions **Essential Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in software engineering, electronics engineering or related technology area * At least 5 years relevant software engineering experience in a high performing team * Strong, demonstrable knowledge or skills in the following: + Cloud Platforms: - AWS/GCP - Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) - Container orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker Compose) + CI/CD Tools: - Jenkins, BitBucket, Gitlab, GitHub Actions - ArgoCD, FluxCD - Source control (Git) + Configuration Management: - Ansible, Terraform - Shell scripting - Infrastructure automation + Monitoring \& Logging: - Prometheus, Grafana, Logi, Alloy - Application performance monitoring tools * Knowledge of code versioning tools (Git), agile methodology, and industry standard development tools * Strong communication skills and ability to work well in a team * Excellent problem solving, coding, testing, and debugging skills * Strong track record of shipping products, features and functionality on time, at extremely high quality * Ability to quickly learn and ramp up on new technologies * Passion for learning and driven to succeed * Ready for a challenge Desirable Requirements * Masters Degree in Software Engineering or a related field * Prior experience in a startup environment **About The Company** At Horizon Quantum Computing, we are developing tools to simplify and expedite the process of developing software for quantum computers, a fundamentally new form of computer that exploits effects from quantum physics to process information more efficiently. We are an international team headquartered in Singapore, with key markets spread out worldwide, and a growing office in Dublin, Ireland.

title: Electrical Engineering Specialist
description: **Senior Electrical Owners Engineer (Engineering Design Services / Construction Transmission Projects (Substation, HV cable and Overhead Line Design)** Reporting to: **Client Engineering Manager** Location: **Dublin/ Cork: Hybrid** (candidates can be located around Ireland, with pay expenses for travel) Type: **Permanent Or Contractor** (Initial Contract Duration: 12\+ months) Job Description This role sits within the **Engineering \& Asset Management** team and is responsible for delivering the **Senior Electrical Owners Engineer** s function across **Transmission Projects.** The successful candidate will ensure that projects are developed in line with client’s statutory obligations, standards and policies. The **Client Engineering Function** includes **Design Reviews, Site Inspections,** and **Pre\-Commissioning** and **Commissioning Inspections** for acceptance on both contestable and non\-contestable **Transmission Projects,** ensuring compliance with functional specifications and standards. A key aspect of the role is the preparation and issue of functional and **Technical Specifications** to customers connecting to the **Transmission System** , together with ongoing engagement to resolve technical queries. This is essential to ensure that all **connections** meet **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** specifications, operational requirements, and relevant national and international standards. These customer connections are strategically important to **Ireland’s** **Renewable Energy** ambitions and to supporting sustainable economic growth. The successful candidate will also perform the **Senior Electrical Owners Engineer** s role for **Transmission Reinforcement** and network expansion projects delivered by **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** and **Distribution System/Networks Operator (DNO)** The ideal candidate will bring strong expertise in **Substation** , **HV cable** and **Overhead Line Design** , together with the ability to assess **Project Designs, Construction Activities** and **Commissioning Phases** for compliance with standards and requirements for **High Voltage Alternating Current projects** . The role also requires the effective management of technical queries throughout project delivery, ensuring they are addressed promptly and in accordance with **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** standards and policies. A key part of the position is the application of sound technical judgement and problem\-solving capability to ensure that appropriate technical standards are consistently achieved across projects. Key Responsibilities Ensure that technical queries raised during projects are managed in accordance with **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** standards and policies and are resolved efficiently. Ensure that projects are delivered in a manner that meets **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** requirements. Review and assess submitted detailed designs against **Ireland’s Electricity Transmission System Operator** **(TSO)** functional requirements, standard designs, and relevant national and international standards. Undertake on\-site inspection and monitoring of **Construction Progress** to ensure compliance with company standards, approved **Detailed Designs** , and relevant national and international standards. Undertake **On\-Site Inspection** and monitoring of **Pre\-Commissioning** activities to ensure compliance with company standards and relevant national and international standards. Verify that all **Transmission System** requirements have been satisfied to enable **Commissioning** tests to proceed. Ensure that developments are commissioned by the TAO in compliance with their requirements through the following activities: Prepare and issue **Commissioning Instructions.** Monitor and audit **Commissioning** activities against **Commissioning Instructions** and relevant national and international standards. **Lead Engagement** with customer and **Stakeholder Technical Consultants,** as required, to resolve issues arising at different stages of the project. Coordinate with peers to support business priorities, manage emerging issues, provide technical guidance, and act as an escalation point for the **Client Engineering Manager.** Manage project interactions with **Client Engineering Vendors, Customers,** and internal **Stakeholders.** Provide technical direction and support across the organisation on assigned projects, including guidance to team members and junior staff, and act as a team representative when required. Ensure completion of Stage Gate Reviews for all customer projects to confirm compliance with functional specifications, and capture and share lessons learned following project energisation. Contribute to reviews and support the introduction of new tools and technologies, as well as the development and implementation of procedures required for the effective delivery of **Transmission Projects.** Lead engagement and coordination with **Distribution System/Networks Operator (DNO)** , including meetings, reporting, relationship management, and general communications relevant to the client engineering role. Represent in Factory Acceptance Test for **HV equipment** part of his projects scope as required. Support and contribute to project reviews, assessments, and audits conducted by internal and external parties, as required. Prepare and present reports and project approval papers for the Executive, the **Grid Infrastructure Projects Committee,** and the Board, as required, within strict deadlines. Prepare relay settings request and Energisation Instruction request for the assigned projects. This request is sent operation to produce these documents. Knowledge, Skills and Experience * **Degree in Electrical or Electronic Engineering, with a minimum of 6–8 years’** experience in the delivery of **HVAC transmission assets.** * Demonstrated expertise in **Transmission Substation Design** , including experience assessing **Onshore Substation Designs** for compliance with standards at **110/220/400 kV.** * Excellent communication skills, with a strong emphasis on high\-quality report writing and the ability to communicate technical issues clearly and effectively. * Experience working with and implementing **CENELEC standards** relevant to **Substation Design, Overhead Lines,** and **Power Cable Systems.** * Experience ensuring that **TAO, Generator, Demand and IPP customers** are provided with the required functional and **Technical Standards.** * Experience in carrying out on\-site inspection and monitoring of **Construction and Commissioning** activities to ensure compliance and readiness for **Connection** to the **Transmission System.** * Experience in preparing and issuing **Transmission Design Drawings** , including **Single Line Diagrams, Substation Layout Drawings, Protection Schematics** , and interlocking documents. * Proven ability to track and monitor key tasks, take ownership of deliverables, and lead and influence others towards a common vision while working collaboratively across teams. * A proactive and engaging approach, with the drive and enthusiasm to contribute to delivery of the company strategy. * Strong understanding of stakeholder and industry requirements relating to the **Transmission System** and associated developments. * Ability to coach and mentor colleagues to support the delivery of a high\-quality client engineering service. * Proven ability to provide technical input to **Project Managers** , while tracking key tasks and taking ownership of technical deliverables. * Excellent interpersonal, analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong commercial awareness, combined with effective **Vendor Management Capability** . * **Experience Managing Consultancy Engineering Vendors** to ensure effective and efficient **Delivery Of Engineering Services** , including design review and site monitoring. * Full driving licence. If you have a minimum experience requirement is **6\-8 years – and are an Electrical or Electronic Engineer** based any where in Ireland and have a readiness to travel to office locations in **Dublin or Cork,** we would like to hear from you **hello@enexa\-partners.com**

title: Fiduciary Monitoring Supervisor
description: Fiduciary Monitoring Supervisor (Maternity Cover) About the Role An opportunity has arisen for an experienced Fiduciary Monitoring professional to join a leading global financial services organisation on a maternity cover contract. The successful candidate will join a high\-performing team responsible for delivering Depositary and Trustee oversight services to a broad range of regulated and unregulated investment funds. This role offers excellent exposure to investment fund oversight, regulatory compliance, investment restriction monitoring, NAV review processes, cash flow monitoring, and client relationship management within a dynamic and growing business. Key Responsibilities Fiduciary Oversight * Act as a key point of contact in the delivery of Depositary and Trustee oversight services to investment fund clients. * Perform investment and borrowing restriction monitoring across designated funds, ensuring compliance with applicable regulatory requirements and fund documentation. * Monitor investment management activities including securities lending, OTC and exchange\-traded derivatives, and third\-party held assets. * Complete cash flow monitoring activities and investigate, escalate and track any issues identified. * Assist with the onboarding and setup of new funds from an investment monitoring, NAV review, cash flow monitoring and asset register perspective. * Support oversight of operational service providers and ensure agreed service standards are maintained. * Engage directly with clients and service providers regarding investment restriction breaches and other fiduciary\-related matters. * Manage the flow of information between internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure timely resolution of outstanding issues. Projects \& Change Initiatives * Participate in regulatory change projects, process improvement initiatives and operational enhancement programmes. * Contribute to the development and standardisation of fiduciary oversight processes across jurisdictions. * Support additional projects and responsibilities as required. Regulatory \& Industry Knowledge * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of UCITS, AIFMD and other relevant regulations impacting the funds industry. * Monitor industry developments and regulatory changes to ensure oversight activities remain aligned with best practice. Experience \& Skills Required Essential * Minimum 3 years' experience within Fiduciary Services, Depositary Services, Investment Compliance, Fund Operations or a related financial services environment. * Strong understanding of Trustee and Depositary responsibilities. * Knowledge of investment funds and financial instruments. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities. * Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining attention to detail. * Advanced Microsoft Office skills, particularly Excel. Desirable * Experience with investment restriction monitoring and exception\-based monitoring systems. * Knowledge of UCITS and/or AIFMD investment restrictions. * Experience within fund administration, custody, asset servicing, trustee/depositary or investment compliance environments. * Exposure to derivatives, securities lending and complex investment strategies. * VBA and/or SQL experience. Personal Attributes * Strong integrity and professional judgement. * Collaborative team player with a positive and proactive approach. * Self\-motivated and capable of working independently. * Ability to take ownership of tasks and see them through to completion. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong verbal and written communication skills. What's on Offer This position provides an excellent opportunity to gain broad exposure across the investment funds industry, working closely with investment managers, fund administrators, custodians and other key market participants. The successful candidate will develop expertise across fiduciary oversight, regulatory compliance, investment monitoring and operational risk within a highly regarded global organisation.

title: Field Service - Early Career Engineers
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Salary: €36,000\.00 \- €49,500\.00 Location: Leixlip,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. **Field Service Engineer\- Early Career/University Graduates** Kick\-start your engineering career with worldclass training, hands on technical work, and international experience at Applied Materials—the global leader in semiconductor technology. This role is ideal for upcoming 2026 graduates, recent graduates or early\-career engineers who value precision, discipline, and structured learning, and who are adaptable enough to succeed in very different cultural and operational environments. **What you will do as a New College Graduate:** Field Service Engineers (FSE) are working in a state\-of\-the\-art facility and at our customer site in Leixlip, Co. Kildare. where they are the face of Applied Materials and an integral part of a vibrant and diverse team. They like to troubleshoot hardware, solve complex problems, and make equipment work better. **Hands\-On Engineering Work** * Learn to install, maintain, and troubleshoot advanced semiconductor equipment with high\-complex machine components * Work closely with experienced engineers in a cleanroom, high precision environment based on procedures for (un\-)scheduled and predictive maintenance including continuous improvement programs (CIPs) * Use digital diagnostic tools to solve real technical problems by machine data based predictive maintenance approaches (including AI) * Support customer operations and ensure a high\-quality equipment performance with a zero\-defect culture and high consciousness for employee safety and product quality * Build deep technical skills through structured, hands\-on learning on highly specialised Applied Materials equipment applications **Global Training Experience** You’ll participate in an immersive training program at our state\-of\-the\-art facility in one of our global training centers (Asia or the US), where you’ll gain: * Strong foundations in semiconductor equipment * Step by step technical training * Experience working in a highly structured, process driven environment * International exposure and cross\-cultural teamwork All travel and accommodation are fully supported. **We are looking for you:** * You hold, or are on track to complete (in 2026\), a relevant bachelor’s or master’s degree * Basic mechanical aptitude of pneumatics, hydraulics, electronics, vacuum, or thermodynamics * Experience diagnosing and resolving basic technical challenges * Familiarity with hand tools and their appropriate usage, including digital multimeters * Ability to read and interpret electrical and mechanical schematics * Familiarity with hand tools and their appropriate usage, including digital multimeters * Effective written and verbal communication skills * Clear communication skills and the ability to follow structured procedures * Willingness to travel internationally and work in a cleanroom environment * Be flexible to work both day and night shifts **Your mindset is important:** * Team player with strong interpersonal skills and a building trust nature. * Exercise judgement based on data analysis of multiple sources of information like plant documentations, schematics, and hardware data. * Find solutions with different tools to get the machine running again. * Explain difficult or sensitive information and works to build consensus. **Physical Requirements:** * Various physical movements such as bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, and extended walking and standing * Ability to distinguish between colors * Ability to operate hand and power tools * Ability to work in confined spaces * Ability to work in a noise\-prone environment (with consideration for noise sensitivity) * Wear personal protective equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields, particularly in clean room settings * Flexibility for rotational shifts, including nights, weekends, and holidays as required by customer needs **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** New College Grad **Travel:** Yes, 20% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No The salary offered to a selected candidate will be based on multiple factors including location, hire grade, job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, and with consideration of internal equity of our current team members. In addition to a comprehensive benefits package, candidates may be eligible for other forms of compensation such as participation in a bonus and a stock award program, as applicable. For all sales roles, the posted salary range is the Target Total Cash (TTC) range for the role, which is the sum of base salary and target bonus amount at 100% goal achievement. title: Field Service Engineer 3
description: **The group you’ll be a part of** The Customer Support Business Group focuses on enabling our customers with premier customer support throughout their lifecycle with Lam. We drive performance, productivity, safety, and quality of customers installed base performance and deliver service and lifecycle solutions for their most critical equipment and processes. **The impact you’ll make** As a Field Service Engineer at Lam, you will step into the limelight of customer support. Your work goes beyond installations and troubleshooting; it ensures the operational excellence of our complex semiconductor equipment. You're on the front lines, understanding customer needs and collaborating with various teams to deliver solutions. This is a full time position, based in Kildare, Ireland. **What You’ll Do** * Provide quality on\-site repair, maintenance service, and complete installation/relocation start\-up services of Lam’s complex electro\-mechanical and electronic systems at customer sites. * Perform analytical problem\-solving, troubleshooting, conduct diagnostics, and isolate issues to the component level, and take corrective actions to minimize downtime, system interruptions, and equipment malfunctions. * Keep up to date on semiconductor capital equipment and Lam products through learning, training, and certification. * Assist in complex field modifications and updates, maintaining records and reports to coordinate activities between Lam and the customer. * Serve as a subject matter expert, representing Lam to our valued customers, delivering exceptional customer satisfaction. * Troubleshoot and create customer response action plans to support customer needs. * Transparently discuss opportunities and challenges with customer to increase trust. * Provide technical instruction and mentoring for more junior engineers. **Minimum Qualifications** **Who we’re looking for** * Bachelor’s degree in Electronics, Engineering, or related field and 5\+ years of experience; or Master’s degree with 3\+ years; or equivalent work experience. * Previous experience in installation, maintenance, repair, or troubleshooting of semiconductor capital equipment. * Proficiency in interpreting electrical and mechanical schematics, diagrams, and operation manuals. * Able to work in a clean room environment while wearing personal protective safety equipment, or cleanroom suit. * Frequently position oneself (e.g., bend, stoop, reach, walk, sit, twist, squat, kneel, climb, stand) to perform job tasks for extended periods. * Able to travel frequently, domestically and internationally, based on customer needs, flexibility to work a variety shifts including compressed and alternative workweeks including overtime and on\-call if needed. **Preferred Qualifications** * Proficiency in basic computer skills and familiarity with common office software including Microsoft Office Suite. * Communication skills, both written and verbal, in English. * Foundational analytical and problem\-solving skills. **Our commitment** We believe it is important for every person to feel valued, included, and empowered to achieve their full potential. By bringing unique individuals and viewpoints together, we achieve extraordinary results. Lam Research ("Lam" or the "Company") is an equal opportunity employer. Lam is committed to and reaffirms support of equal opportunity in employment and non\-discrimination in employment policies, practices and procedures on the basis of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions), gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, sexual orientation, or military and veteran status or any other category protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws. It is the Company's intention to comply with all applicable laws and regulations. Company policy prohibits unlawful discrimination against applicants or employees. *Lam offers a variety of work location models based on the needs of each role. Our hybrid roles combine the benefits of on\-site collaboration with colleagues and the flexibility to work remotely and fall into two categories – On\-site Flex and Virtual Flex. ‘On\-site Flex’ you’ll work 3\+ days per week on\-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, with the opportunity to work remotely for the balance of the week. ‘Virtual Flex’ you’ll work 1\-2 days per week on\-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, and remotely the rest of the time.*

title: Financial Planner
description: Job Title: Senior Financial Planner Location: Sandyford, Dublin Company: Financial Planning Matters \& BeatTheBank.ie Reporting to: Senior Management Team About Financial Planning Matters: Financial Planning Matters is a leading financial planning firm in Ireland, renowned for its commitment to delivering comprehensive, holistic financial advice. Our approach is centred on addressing the complete financial needs of our clients, ensuring that each financial strategy is tailored to their long\-term objectives. As a forward\-thinking firm, we are constantly innovating to remain at the forefront of the financial planning landscape, and we are seeking a highly qualified professional to join our dynamic team. **Role Overview:** We are recruiting an experienced Senior Financial Planner to join our team. In this critical role, you will work closely with the advice team and senior leadership to implement and enhance our holistic financial planning model. The successful candidate will deliver bespoke financial solutions using advanced planning tools and technology to support individual \& corporate clients in achieving their financial goals. This role is perfect for a self\-starter who thrives in a dynamic environment, with a strong passion for financial planning and a proven ability to manage complex financial situations. You will play a key part in delivering personalised financial advice, ensuring that the firm continues to lead the way in Ireland’s financial planning sector. **Key Roles and Responsibilities:** Comprehensive Financial Planning: Develop and deliver complete financial plans that cover investment strategies, tax efficiency, retirement planning, estate planning, and risk management. You will ensure that every financial strategy is built to meet the client’s holistic needs. Client Relationship Management: Build and maintain strong, trusted relationships with high\-net\-worth clients, providing ongoing financial advice and support. Your ability to listen to client needs and craft tailored solutions will be key to your success. Leadership and Strategic Contribution: You will work in the advice team and with senior management to shape the direction of the firm’s financial planning strategy, ensuring it aligns with the long\-term goals of the business and keeps pace with industry developments. Advanced Financial Modelling: Utilise cutting\-edge financial planning software, such as Voyant and CashCalc, to create detailed cash flow forecasts and financial models. Your expertise in using these tools will ensure the delivery of top\-tier advice and planning services. Compliance and Professional Standards: Ensure that all advice and services are delivered in full compliance with Irish financial regulations, maintaining the highest standards of ethics and professionalism. Team Development: Provide mentorship to junior financial planners, sharing your knowledge and expertise to help them grow and maintain the high standards of the firm. Key Requirements: QFA or Certified Financial Planner (CFP) qualification or working towards this accreditation A minimum of 5 years’ experience in financial planning, with a strong background in working with HENRYs (High Earners Nor Rich Yet) Extensive knowledge of Irish financial landscape, tax planning, retirement planning, investment strategies, and estate planning. Proven proficiency in using financial planning software, particularly Voyant and CashCalc, with the ability to create comprehensive financial plans and mapping out solutions/scenarios. Self\-starter with a demonstrated ability to work independently, take initiative, and lead projects from inception to completion. Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex financial issues for clients. Excellent attention to detail and a results\-driven approach, with the ability to handle complex financial scenarios and problem\-solve effectively. **Preferred Attributes:** A proven ability to leverage technology and software to enhance financial planning services. Strong leadership qualities, with a collaborative approach and a proactive mindset. A commitment to ongoing professional development, staying informed of the latest industry trends and advancements. **What We Offer:** A competitive package of €120,000 (OTE) with performance\-based bonuses based on professional behaviours and top class relationship management traits. Hybrid working environment \- 2 days in our Sandyford Office 5% employer pension contribution, Death\-In\-Service \& Income Protection Opportunities for ongoing professional development and career progression to "Head of Advice" A dynamic, innovative, and supportive working environment that values collaboration. The chance to work closely with industry leaders and shape the future of financial planning in Ireland.

title: Financial Planning Analyst
description: Barden is delighted to have been exclusively engaged by our client to identify an FP\&A Analyst to join their team on a full\-time permanent basis. This is a key appointment for the business as this individual will be a key member of a high\-performing FP\&A team, delivering impactful insights, reporting, and analysis to support strategic decision\-making. This is an exciting opportunity to drive automation, enhance reporting capabilities, and leverage tools such as Power BI and advanced Excel in a fast\-paced, collaborative environment **.** **ABOUT THE ROLE** * Prepare monthly management reports, dashboards, and KPI analysis to support business performance. * Lead budgeting, forecasting, cash flow management, and financial planning activities. * Monitor financial performance, identifying risks, opportunities, and key business trends. * Develop financial models and provide commercial insights to support strategic decision\-making. * Deliver ad hoc financial analysis and recommendations to senior management. * Partner with Finance and operational teams to provide accurate reporting and influence business decisions. * Drive continuous improvement through automation, process enhancement, and adoption of best practices and emerging technologies. **ABOUT THE PERSON:** * Qualified or part\-qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) with experience in FP\&A. * Strong financial, analytical, and Excel skills, with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights. * Proven track record in reporting automation, process improvement, and data quality enhancement. * Effective communicator with strong stakeholder management and business partnering skills. * Commercially minded with a continuous improvement mindset; experience with financial planning systems is advantageous.

title: Food Safety and Quality Graduate
description: Overview: **Company:** Dawn Meats **Location:** Slane Co. Meath **Reporting to:** Technical Manager **Contract type:** Full\-Time/Permanent **Role Summary:** This is a fantastic graduate level opportunity for a technically focused individual to represent the company on all technical issues, liasing between R\&D, sales and the production area. Ensuring that all products are produced in a safe and legal manner and in compliance with Dawn Meats best practice. Ensuring that all products are in alignment with both the company's policies and the retailer's policies and strategies. Your role will be key as part of the Technical team in the delivery and efficient implementation of Technical practices and procedures. Responsibilities: **The successful candidate will be based in our Slane production plant in Co. Meath and will be responsible for:*** Having an awareness of food science and nutrition with a focus on best practice and from a legislative perspective \- Food safety, hygiene and nutritional requirements * Assisting with quality operations, procedures and document control * Maintaining and updating the department files including SOPs, risk assessments, HACCP documents and quality management documents * Carrying out audits as per schedule * Evaluating the written SOP's being carried out * Maintaining and managing non\-conformance logs * Logging complaints and communicating to the relevant personnel * Assisting with close out investigations into complaints including root cause analysis * Microbiological and chemical sampling of finished product and raw materials as per sampling schedule * Carrying out internal audits and assisting with external and customer audits Qualifications: **Your role will see you tackle a wide range of business challenges in a busy technical environment.** **The ideal Candidate will have or demonstrate:*** A degree in a related discipline (Food Science, Agricultural Science, Nutrition, Science) * Proficient in Microsoft word/excel/outlook etc. Decisiveness and the ability to work well as part of a team * Strong attention to detail * Commercial awareness * Excellent communication skills \- Oral \& written * HACCP trained is an advantage INDLP

title: Food and Beverage Assistant
description: **Job Description** The **Conrad Dublin** is a 5\* hotel and part of the Hilton Luxury Division. We aim to ‘offer smart luxury travelers inspiring connections \& intuitive service in a world of style’. We are one of the best 5\* luxury hotels in the country. *“Conrad is Empowering and Bold”* **Benefits** Working for Hilton means you are joining an international company with more than 9000 hotels across the globe with countless benefits \& career development opportunities such as: * Travel \- Up to 40 nights per year at discounted rates and 50% off F\&B in Hilton Hotels around the world. Plus up to 70 nights of discounts for your Family and Friends * Team Member Gym – a dedicated team member gym with free access 7 days a week * Benefit – Discounted rates for Conrad Team members across a wide range of retailers * Development – Hilton have numerous learning \& development plans for all types of roles and career paths as well as free access to LinkedIn Learning and Harvard Manage Mentor * Holidays – 30 days holiday (incl BH) plus long service entitlement of an extra day per year up to a total of 5 additional days * Maternity \& Paternity – Paid leave for those that qualify **Responsibilities** Here's what you'll do during a typical day: * Support all F\&B operations: Work across bar, restaurant, room service, and events * Delight our guests: Greet guests warmly, take and serve orders accurately, and ensure a consistently high level of service * Prepare tables with precision: Set and arrange tables for dining and events, including folding napkins, and cleaning and placing tableware * Ensure cleanliness and safety: Keep work areas clean and organized * Comply with regulatory standards: Maintain compliance with health, safety, sanitation, and alcohol awareness regulations, ensuring the highest standards of guest and team member well\-being **Please note this is a PM shift.** **Qualifications** **What It Takes to Make the Stay** At Hilton, Our Core Values Define What It Takes To Succeed Here And Guide The Qualities We Look For In Every Team Member A passion for spreading the light and warmth of **Hospitality** . Acting with **Integrity** and always doing the right thing. Inspiring others through **Leadership** . A belief that **Teamwork** drives the best outcomes. A sense of **Ownership** and accountability. And a focus on the **Now** , bringing urgency and discipline to every moment, knowing it can make a lasting impact. **About Us** **Join an Award\-Winning Workplace Culture** At Hilton, we don’t just deliver exceptional experiences for our guests—we build an exceptional workplace for the Team Members who make it all possible. As a global leader in hospitality, we’ve welcomed more than 3 billion guests worldwide, all while staying true to our founding vision: to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality. Our award\-winning culture has earned us repeated recognition on the World’s Best Workplaces list by Great Place to Work and Fortune. With our suite of world\-class brands , and a company\-wide commitment to providing the best stay for every guest, we’re setting new standards for the future of travel. Whether you’re starting your career or exploring something new, Hilton supports your journey every step of the way. Come for the job, stay for the career—and help us make every stay a little more magical. Curious about life at Hilton? Explore our Careers Blog to see why we’re more than a great place to stay—we’re a great place to work.

title: Fraud MI & Insights Analyst.
description: At AIB, our values guide how we work and how we support each other. We’re looking for someone who puts **Customer First** , takes initiative and **Owns the Outcome** , and is always looking for ways to **Eliminate Complexity** . You’ll treat colleagues and customers with fairness and **Show Respect** , and you’ll thrive in a culture built on collaboration where we **Be One Team** to deliver meaningful impact. Role: Fraud MI \& Insights Analyst Location/Office Policy: Central Park, Dublin 18 \- onsite 3 days a week * Would you like to play a leading role in a team whose ambition is to become a world class fraud function, exceptional at developing talent and turning information into insights? * Do you enjoy collaborating with cross functional teams and influencing business direction with data\-backed evidence? * Do you actively seek opportunities for innovation and continuous improvement? **What Is The Role** We are looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join the Fraud Information Management team within AIB. The role focuses on Data Analysis and designing Power BI dashboards for MI reporting, supporting through data\-driven insights and high\-quality reporting. The ideal candidate will play a key role in transforming Fraud and operational data into clear, actionable dashboards and management information to support decision\-making across Fraud, Business, and Operations teams. **Key accountabilities;** * Analyse Payments, Cards and Operational datasets to identify trends, risks, and performance insights * Design and protoype scalable, automated MI PowerBI dashboards across Payment, Cards and other Fraud domains aligned to Fraud Management, BAU Fraud Operations, Customer Centric, Regulatory Reporting and Operation Status dimensions * Assist Data Tribe in developing the PowerBI dashboards by handing over the data requirements and to build a sustainable MI capability into BAU * Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and alignment with business definitions * Drive automation, standardisation, and continuous improvement in MI and reporting processes * Contribute to building a sustainable, scalable MI \& Insights capability embedded within BAU * Support automation and improvement of reporting processes **What You Will Bring** * 4\-5 years’ experience in Data analysis and MI reporting within Banking or Financial Services * Hands\-on experience using SQL, large datasets and deploying Power BI dashboards * Strong knowledge of data visualisation best practices, including choosing the right charts, KPIs, and interactive features * Ability to simplify complex data into clear, meaningful visuals and management information to support reporting and MI * Analytical mindset to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies and translate them into to turn data into dashboards and business\-friendly insights * Operate in a self\-driven manner, demonstrating the initiative, ownership, and independent problem\-solving, while requiring minimal instruction or oversight * Good to have understanding of fraud typologies (e.g. card fraud, payment fraud, account takeover, mule activity) **Why Work For AIB** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. *Some of our benefits include;* * Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year Please click **here** for further information about AIB’s PACT – Our Commitment to You. **Key Capabilities** * Collaborates * Eliminates Complexity * Develops \& Empowers * Data Insight * Requirement Analysis * Customer Communication If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the Recruiter for this role, Sarah Lyons, at careers@aib.ie for a conversation. AIB is an equal opportunities employer, and we pride ourselves on being the first bank in Ireland to receive the Investors in Diversity Gold Standard accreditation from the Irish Centre for Diversity. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please email the Talent Acquisition team at careers@aib.ie **Disclaimer** Unsolicited CV’s sent to AIB by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this position. AIB operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the Talent Acquisition team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. **Application deadline : Tuesday 14th July 2026**

title: Front Office Analyst (f/m/d)
description: **Workload: 100%** Join us in our mission to expand Axpo's footprint in the Irish energy markets! We are seeking a detail\-oriented Front Office Analyst to support our front office team in Ireland. **What you will do:** * Support Origination: Build and maintain pricing models and perform data analysis to support a range of origination transactions covering power purchase agreements, electricity and gas supply agreements, Irish forward power products, wholesale gas shipping, biomethane, and battery storage. * Perform Daily Analysis: Use tools like Excel, Python, and SQL to support the portfolio management team, analyse P\&L results, monitor risk KPIs, and manage reporting for internal and external stakeholders. * Deal Booking: Ensure timely and accurate entry of trades and contracts into trading and back\-office systems. * Governance: Validate and improve models across origination and portfolio management. * Data Reconciliation: Perform data reconciliation across multiple Axpo platforms and address discrepancies. * Drive Automation: Collaborate across Axpo’s global teams to implement automation and efficiencies in how we handle and manage data within the Irish business. **What you bring \& who you are:** * Prior experience in the wholesale or retail energy market in Ireland is desirable. * Bachelor’s degree (Master’s preferred) in Data Science, Data Analytics, Software Development, Finance, Economics, or Engineering. * Proficiency in analysing data using various tools (e.g., Excel, SQL, Python) and/or dashboarding software (Power BI). * Comfortable working in a small cross\-functional team. **About the team:** We are a dynamic and forward\-thinking team at Axpo, where innovation and collaboration drive our success in the energy sector. Our team is comprised of entrepreneurial professionals with significant experience in the Irish energy markets. We foster a supportive environment where creativity is encouraged, challenges are embraced, and each team member's contributions are valued. By joining us, you'll be part of an experienced team of energy professionals and will have the opportunity to work on a broad range of interesting transactions in a rapidly developing and dynamic energy market. *At Axpo Group, we are dedicated to fostering a culture of non\-discrimination, tolerance, and inclusion. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications regardless of race and ethnicity, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, as well as socioeconomic, cultural, and religious background. We are committed to ensuring a respectful and inclusive recruiting process and workplace for everyone.*
