title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Elusav Recruitment has a great opportunity for an Mechanical engineer with one of Irelands most reputable and largest Engineering contractors based in their Dublin HQ, and supporting local and international D\&B projects. The organisation have recently centralised their engineering team internally, and looking to expand. Reporting to the Central Engineering manager, the Mechanical design Engineer will be responsible for ensuring technical compliance on all project and, management of RIBA3\-5 design processes and procedures. Some duties of the role involve attending design team meetings, managing procurement and technical submittals, assisting with tenders, producing deliverables such as plant replacement schedules, and occasional site visits. This is an excellent opportunity with a well respected and sustainably growing engineering company, building out their Design and Build capabilities whilst are leveraging innovative construction technologies to ensure efficient and high quality delivery. Extensive progression and learning opportunities **Responsibilities:** * Responsible for ensuring technical compliance on all assigned projects. * Responsible for ensuring scope of works are completed for mechanical packages. * Responsible for managing consultant selections and agreeing RACI matrices. * Attending design team meetings, in person or through MSTF teams. * Responsible for ensuring design programme are aligned with planning teams. * Complete reviews of client responsibility matrices and ensure alignment with project/commercial team. * Responsible for management of RIBA3\-5 design processes and procedures. * Responsible for producing design management plan for projects. * Responsible for ensuring Central engineering checklists are implemented on each project. * Management of design for safe manufacturing assembly if implemented when required on the projects. * Responsible for ensuring below trackers are managed correctly from start\-handover of project: Procurement schedule; Technical submittal register (CSA); Drawing register (CSA); RFI log; Deviation tracker; Lessons learned log; Design Change Log; Monitor all schedule and trackers throughout the course of assigned project; Carry out technical submittal \& working drawing reviews with the teams on a scheduled basis. * Assist with workload on the projects you are involved with (Technical submittals, Drawing reviews, RFI's etc). * Assist the project teams with better buying \- Review orders and ensure best price is obtained. * Challenge the consultants and Company teams to value engineer. * Responsible for enring compliance with ISO19650 requirements. * Produce report for the projects you are assigned to and issue to direct manager at agreed periods. * Assist with tenders for new projects when required. * Assisting the estimating dept and pre\-construction team at agreed periods. * Assist with setting up the value engineering log for the DC\&BS group. * Attend Central Engineering stage 5 weekly model review meeting. * Responsible for ensuring the project undergoes a lessons learned review at design stage. * Coaching graduate engineers on projects, how to produce tech subs, manage drawing production, issuing of RFI's, dealing with clients. * Attend projects within Europe for site visits from time to time. * Attend monthly Central Engineering F2F meeting. * All other tasks related to Design role that is requested by your direct line manager. **Requirements:** * Third level qualification in Mechanical Engineering/ Mechanical Design Engineering. * 3\+ years experience working on large industrial plant projects. * Proficiency in a related Mechanical Design package/ software. * Experience working on datacentres would be an advantage but not essential. **Contact:** If you would like to learn more about the vacancy, apply now, or contact Brendan Mullins at bmullins@elusav.com or \+353 61 527 150\.

title: Mechanical Estimator
description: Elusav Recruitment have an exciting opportunity for a Mechanical Estimator with one of the most reputable and largest Engineering contractors in Europe. Based in Dublin. The role will involve preparing detailed estimates and competitive tenders for large\-scale Data Centre projects across Europe. You would be responsible for reviewing drawings and specifications, managing quantities and pricing, coordinating with subcontractors and suppliers, identifying project risks, and working closely with both Mechanical, Electrical and CSA teams throughout the tender process. **Responsibilities:** * Produce reports for management for bids and monthly meetings. * Prepare estimates based on drawings and specifications. * Manage quantities and prepare pricing document. * Oversea pricing for elements of the building from the Irish and European subcontractors and suppliers. * Utilise spreadsheets to compare subcontractors bids and quotations, and to demonstrate reasoning for selecting subcontractors. * Assist in the maintenance of the cost data base. * Present the estimate to the Estimating Director, and to the Board of Directors, where and when required. * Build relationships with key vendors (subcontractors, suppliers, engineers etc). * Keep informed with developments in the construction industry. * Participate in the development of procedures to ensure a high standard of quality control in the documents produced by the Estimating Department. * Co\-operate with the application of quality assurance procedures. * Mentor and train junior members of the team. * Co\-ordinate and collaborate with the CSA \& Electrical Estimators. **Requirements:** * 4\+ years experience within a Mechanical capacity. * Estimating or Quantity Surveying background. * Knowledge of FIDIC contracts is desirable. * Proficiency in the use of Microsoft Word, Excel, and in at least one of the more widely used QS BQ production software packages \- e.g. Buildsoft or CostX. * Understand the requirement to achieve a high level of quality in the production of subcontractor tender documentation and tender submission. **Contact:** If you would like to learn more about the vacancy, apply now, or contact Thaissa Torres at Ttorres@elusav.com or \+353 1 582 2807\.

title: Microsoft Dynamics Consultant
description: My Dublin based client is recruiting for a Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer to join the team on a perm basis working on a hybrid model. The Microsoft Dynamics 365 Developer will work on a large scale transformation programme and you will support delivery across several strategics initiatives. * Deliver and support Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement solutions within customer service\-focused environments. * Configure and enhance Dynamics 365 applications in line with operational and business requirements. * Build and optimise automated workflows using Power Automate to improve efficiency and reduce manual processes. * Support structured release management processes using CI/CD pipelines across multiple environments. * Maintain source control standards and collaborative development practices using Git\-based repositories. * Contribute to technical documentation, environment governance, and integration activities. * Configure and customise core CRM elements including entities/tables, forms, dashboards, views, workflows, business process flows, and security models. * Develop custom extensions using plugins, JavaScript, and related technologies where required. * Assist with the design and support of Power Platform solutions, including Canvas Apps. * Keep current with emerging Microsoft technologies, platform updates, and best practices. **Delivery \& Project Responsibilities** * Contribute to project delivery activities in line with agreed timelines, priorities, and objectives. * Participate in Agile ceremonies and work effectively within sprint\-based delivery models. * Manage assigned tasks proactively and communicate progress, risks, and dependencies to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with technical teams, business users, and project stakeholders to support successful solution delivery. **Skills \& Experience** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience delivering Microsoft Dynamics 365 CE/CRM solutions. * Strong understanding of Dynamics 365 Customer Service and the wider Microsoft Power Platform ecosystem. * Experience designing scalable, maintainable, and reusable technical solutions. * Hands\-on experience developing plugins, workflows, Power Automate solutions, Logic Apps, and Canvas Apps. * Knowledge of Azure integration services and cloud\-based solution design. * Experience working with ALM and DevOps practices, including Git and Azure DevOps. * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills across cloud and enterprise application environments. * Ability to interpret complex business and technical requirements and translate them into effective solutions. * Strong communication and collaboration skills, with experience working across multidisciplinary teams. * Experience with Azure\-based web applications or enterprise system integrations would be beneficial. * Good understanding of SaaS applications, APIs, and integration approaches.

title: National Coach Developer
description: **Job Title:** National Coach Developer **Location:** FAI HQ, Abbottstown or Regional **Direct Reporting Line:** Coach Development Manager **Duration:** Permanent / Full\-Time **Work Model:** Hybrid **Introduction** The Football Association of Ireland (FAI) is the national governing body for football in Ireland, driving the growth, promotion, and development of the game at all levels. We are passionate about inspiring participation, supporting talent, and celebrating the power of football to unite communities across the country. With a proud history and an ambitious vision for the future, the FAI is committed to fostering excellence on and off the pitch, ensuring the game is inclusive, accessible, and a source of national pride. **Purpose of the Role** The **National Coach Developer** will lead the delivery and development of the FAI’s Coach Development programmes, supporting coaches from entry\-level to UEFA licence pathways. The role will create and deliver high\-quality formal and informal learning opportunities, enhance coach skills and knowledge, and contribute to the professional growth of coaches across Ireland. **Key Responsibilities** Coach Development Pathways * Deliver formal coaching courses crossing the full Coach Development Pathway, from entry\-level to UEFA qualifications. * Facilitate engaging, inclusive, and high\-quality learning environments in both classroom and on\-pitch settings. * Provide ongoing mentoring, observation, and feedback to support coaches’ development beyond formal courses. * Develop and maintain informal learning initiatives, including communities of practice, workshops, peer learning, and online forums. Contribute to the design and enhancement of coach development materials, resources, and assessment processes. * Collaboration \& Stakeholder Engagement * Represent the FAI at regional and national events, promoting coaching excellence and development programmes. * Collaborate with Regional FAI staff, tutors, mentors, and player development programmes to ensure alignment across pathways. Build partnerships with educational institutions. * **Person Specification** **Experience and Qualifications** Essential: * UEFA B Licence. * Track record in designing, delivering, and evaluating coach development programmes. * Significant coaching and/or coach development experience. * Experience mentoring or supporting coaches in formal and informal settings. * Excellent communication, facilitation, and relationship\-management skills. * Strong administrative skills and familiarity with relevant IT systems. * Experience managing projects and working collaboratively. * Experience delivering learner\-centred, reality\-based coaching environments. * Strong understanding of the UEFA Coaching Convention and FAI Coach Education framework. Full Clean Driving licence. * Desirable: * UEFA A Licence or higher * Experience as a Coach Developer, Tutor, or Mentor in a recognised association. * QQI Coach Developer Level 7\. * Formal training or qualification in coach education, sports coaching, or adult learning. * Experience using digital learning tools and blended education approaches. Knowledge of the Irish football structure and grassroots\-to\-elite pathways. * **Skills \& Professional Knowledge** * A positive and proactive team player with excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Passionate about supporting coach and player development with a reflective, learner\-centered approach. * Innovative and open to new approaches and technologies in coach education. * Committed to inclusivity, diversity, and excellence in football coaching. * Passionate about helping others grow and succeed in coaching. * Reflective, empathetic, and learner\-centred in approach. * Innovative and open to new ideas and technologies in education. * Excellent collaborator with a team\-focused mindset. * Committed to inclusivity, diversity, and excellence in Irish football. **)** ***(Progress)*** Thinks creatively and with an open mind to identify new opportunities for growth and improvement. Embraces change positively and contribute to building a better future for Irish football. **)** Demonstrates energy, enthusiasm, and pride in everything they do. Takes initiative, delivers results, and strives to make a meaningful impact on the game and the organisation. ***(Togetherness)*** Works effectively and respectfully with others to achieve shared goals. Supports, challenges, and motivates colleagues to perform at their best, recognising that success is achieved together. ***(Togetherness / Passion)*** Communicates clearly, openly, and respectfully with colleagues and stakeholders. Builds positive relationships, inspires trust, and represents the FAI with professionalism and integrity. **)** Takes ownership of performance and upholds high standards in all tasks. Acts responsibly, follows through on commitments, and contributes to the FAI’s vision of continuous progress and excellence. **Our Benefits** We are proud of the range of benefits we can provide: * Two premium level tickets to each home international game * Sick pay scheme and family friendly leave * Opportunities to work at international matches * Company pension * Life assurance * Bike to work scheme * Free onsite parking * Employee assistance programs * Hybrid working arrangements and flexible hours working arrangements * Discounted access to the National Aquatic Centre and gym complex **Application Details** Closing date for receipt of applications is close of business on **Friday 10th July 2026\.** Shortlisting and interviewing process will take place thereafter. **The Football Association of Ireland is an equal opportunities employer and promotes Equity, Diversity \& Inclusion (ED\&I).**

title: Network Design Engineer
description: Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **Dublin, Ireland; London, UK**.### **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related field, or equivalent practical experience. * 3 years of experience with network routing protocols, design and troubleshooting, with network equipment providers. * 3 years of experience in Dense Wavelength Division Multiplexing (DWDM) or Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol (TCP/IP) or Network system design operations. * Experience in one or more programming languages such as C\+\+, Go, or Python. * Experience in coding/scripting languages such as Go or Python, and SQL scripts. * Experience with Content Distribution Networks (CDN), IP transit/peering, dark fiber, colocation, optical transport, and cloud technologies. ### **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience with planning and designing networks (e.g., multiple sites, multiple products, etc.). * Experience in telecommunications at carrier\-class scale, with experience of DWDM optical and IP networks, as well as network automation scripting using Go or Python, and SQL scripts. * Experience with scaled capacity augment/migration projects in telecommunication, hyperscalar, data center or colocation networking environment. * Experience with defining system or process requirements, building, integrating, or delivering layer 1, layer 2 or layer 3 network systems. * Experience with forecasting and planning, and design tools, workflow automation tooling and improvement, automation scripts. **About the job** ----------------- Google is proud to boast a network that provides service to millions of Internet users around the world. The Network Engineering team is responsible for operating that network reliably and at scale. As a member of the team, you have a direct impact on design and feature enhancements to keep our systems running smoothly. You also ensure that network operations are safe and efficient by monitoring network performance, coordinating planned maintenance, adjusting hardware components and responding to network connectivity issues. Google's complex network generates a constant stream of challenges which require you to continually be innovative with an evolving set of technologies. Keeping the network reliable ensures that our users stay connected with our suite of applications, products and services. In this role, you will plan and design the world's largest Google's production global network. You will be involved in a series of dynamic capacity augments, migrations, and new technology introductions globally to transition to Alphanet. You will execute and build upon our goal of an incredible optical, edge, core and campus network infrastructure for Alphanet to support continuous growing customer and ML demand. You will ensure that the network is efficiently built on\-time while meeting our reliability and policy requirements. You will develop network planning and design best practices, processes and tooling. You will promote, create and contribute to automation tooling/scripts into network planning and design. You will drive the software automation roadmap to improve the quality and reduce the cycle time for network planning and design.Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next\-generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €100000 \- €103000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Learn more about benefits at Google.**Responsibilities** -------------------- * Review Google's network architecture standards and design global infrastructure that meets capacity requirements and service\-level objectives (SLOs) while optimizing the capital expenditures and operating expenditures (CapEx/OpEx) balance. * Incorporate newly New Product Introduction (NPI)’d solutions into plans and designs, ensure planning and design toolsets and business processes are ready for NPIs. * Evolve the business processes throughout their life\-cycle (e.g., input gathering, generation, validation, publication, handoff). * Support establishment and maintenance of business practices and policies in network planning and design (e.g., architectural/topology, hardware life\-cycle, capacity/utilization target), improve performance, reliability, and reduce risk. * Create and promote automation tooling/scripts into network planning and design, contribute to the automation roadmap of all automatable tasks performed by the team, substantially reducing cycle time and improving quality and consistency. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Network Engineer, Deployment & Support
description: Meta is looking for a Network Deployment \& Support Engineer who will be responsible for the deployment and support of one of the world’s largest and most complex networks. As a member of this team, you will have a unique opportunity to be involved in projects related to implementation \& scaling of Meta’s network. You will need to work with a constant sense of urgency continually growing a constantly evolving network. This team is responsible for planning and scaling one of the world's largest backbone and edge networks. As a Network Deployment Engineer, you will propose new ideas, gather constructive feedback from others, and push forward to make these ideas a reality. Network Engineer, Deployment \& Support Responsibilities: * Deploy and support our global production including edge, backbone, data center, campus, and content delivery network (CDN) infrastructure * Work cross\-functionally with project management, network engineering to generate implementation plans including Method of Procedure (MOP) and Bill of Materials (BOM), Engineering Design Packages (EDP), Scope of Works (SOW), Network equipment and server Rack Face Elevations (RFE) * Troubleshoot and resolve complex network issues, collaborate as needed to ensure timely resolution * Provide technical leadership and guidance during network deployment and migration activities in our data centers and POPs, including in the areas of facility power, cooling, rack layout and cable management, physical equipment installation, network equipment configuration, testing and handoff for production traffic * Drive problem management initiatives to solve complex issues that span multiple organizations. Initiatives exist globally across technical infrastructure reliability, policy, security, compliance, and business strategy * Help drive and align on workflow management tool requirements \- efficiency and automation roadmaps * Manage key vendor relationships including contract negotiations, pricing, and evaluation of current and potential partnerships * Manage partner engineering team relationships to enable the team(s) to effectively meet business needs * Develop and maintain technical documentation for network infrastructure and services * Provide input to the scaling and evolution of the team to meet the projected network demands * Up to 10% to 30% of travel Minimum Qualifications: * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience * 5\+ years of IP networking experience with deploying, migrating and supporting service providers, data center, and/or enterprise network infrastructure * Experience configuring and troubleshooting IP routing/switching technologies and protocols including TCP/IP, BGP, ISIS, MPLS, RSVP and SDN * Experience coordinating and executing hardware installation and configuration * Hands\-on experience with enterprise and carrier class routing, switching, and optical transport platforms * Knowledge of data center design and operational best practices * title: PLATFORM SUPPORT ENGINEER
description: **FULL TIME** **Dublin, Ireland** Réalta Technologies are searching for a Platform Support Engineer to support our Pharmaceutical client based in Dublin. This candidate will provide technical ownership and ongoing support for manufacturing digital platforms within a regulated pharmaceutical environment. The successful candidate will act as a key interface between Automation, MES, IT and Operations teams to ensure reliable integration, performance and continuous improvement of site manufacturing systems. The role will support the full system lifecycle including platform administration, equipment integration, change management, qualification activities and troubleshooting of manufacturing information systems. **Job Responsibilities** * Provide technical support and ownership for manufacturing platforms including MES, OPC interfaces, industrial data historians and integrated automation systems. * Support the integration of process equipment and control systems with MES and site digital platforms using OPC and related technologies. * Configure, maintain and troubleshoot MES interfaces, data collection systems and historian solutions such as AVEVA PI. * Lead and support change control activities, including impact assessments, implementation planning and documentation updates. * Develop, execute and review qualification documentation, including test protocols, commissioning activities and validation deliverables in accordance with GMP requirements. * Investigate and resolve platform issues, performing root cause analysis and implementing sustainable corrective actions to ensure system reliability. * Collaborate with Automation, CSV, IT, Engineering and Operations teams to support new equipment integrations, upgrades and site projects. * Support the lifecycle management of manufacturing systems, including patching, upgrades, periodic reviews and obsolescence planning. * Maintain system documentation, including design specifications, configuration records, SOPs and support procedures. * Identify and implement continuous improvement initiatives to enhance data integrity, system performance and manufacturing efficiency. **Job Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in Automation, Electrical, Computer, Chemical Engineering, Information Systems, or a related technical discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience supporting manufacturing, automation, or digital platforms within a pharmaceutical or other GMP\-regulated environment. * Experience supporting and integrating Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES) with plant equipment and control systems. * Strong understanding of industrial communication protocols, particularly OPC technologies. * Hands\-on experience with industrial data historians, preferably AVEVA PI. * Knowledge of GMP requirements, computerized system lifecycle management, and change control processes. * Experience authoring and executing qualification and validation documentation, including IQ/OQ protocols and testing activities. * Familiarity with PLC, HMI, and SCADA technologies, with exposure to platforms such as Allen\-Bradley considered advantageous. * Strong troubleshooting, problem\-solving, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work effectively across Automation, IT, Validation, Engineering, and Operations teams.

title: Physiotherapist (Musculoskeletal Rehabilitation & Performance)
description: Physiotherapist (Musculoskeletal Rehabilitation \& Performance) (Location: Stepaside Dublin) Job Type Full Time Description Physiotherapist (Musculoskeletal Rehabilitation \& Performance) **Clinic:** Resync Physiotherapy, Stepaside, Dublin 18 **Contract:** Full\-Time / Part\-Time **Reports to:** Clinical Director \& Senior Clinical Team **About Resync Physiotherapy** At Resync Physiotherapy, we believe rehabilitation should be structured, measurable, and free from guesswork. **Our Philosophy Is Simple** **Assess → Rehabilitate → Perform** We combine expert physiotherapy assessment with structured rehabilitation, strength and conditioning, performance coaching, and objective testing to help people return to the activities they love with confidence. What makes Resync different is our commitment to applying the same principles used in elite sport to every patient who walks through our doors. Whether someone is recovering from surgery, trying to get back to running, managing persistent pain, returning to GAA, or simply wanting to move better and stay active, they receive the same level of clinical reasoning, objective testing, rehabilitation planning, and coaching that would be expected in a high\-performance sporting environment. Our multidisciplinary team includes Clinical Specialist Physiotherapists, Physiotherapists, Athletic Therapists, Strength \& Conditioning Coaches, and Performance Coaches working together under one roof. Supported by a purpose\-built rehabilitation gym and industry\-leading technology, we help active adults, runners, field\-sport athletes, and post\-operative patients achieve meaningful, measurable outcomes. We are passionate about bridging the gap between healthcare and performance, ensuring that every patient benefits from a world\-class rehabilitation experience regardless of their starting point or sporting ability. The Role We are seeking an enthusiastic and motivated Physiotherapist with a minimum of two years' musculoskeletal experience to join our growing team. This role is ideal for a clinician who is passionate about musculoskeletal rehabilitation, enjoys working with active individuals, and wants to continue developing their clinical skills within a supportive and ambitious environment. You will manage your own caseload while receiving mentorship, clinical support, and structured professional development from our senior team. You will play a key role in helping patients move from pain and injury through rehabilitation and back to full function, sport, or performance. At Resync, physiotherapists are more than assessors and manual therapists. We view our clinicians as coaches, problem\-solvers, and rehabilitation specialists who guide patients through every stage of recovery using objective measures, education, exercise, and evidence\-based decision\-making. Key Responsibilities Clinical Care * Assess, diagnose, and manage a wide range of musculoskeletal conditions. * Deliver evidence\-based treatment plans tailored to individual patient goals. * Develop clear rehabilitation programmes focused on restoring strength, movement, confidence, and function. * Educate patients on their condition and provide practical strategies to support recovery. * Recognise when referral, further investigation, or senior clinical input is required. * Maintain accurate and timely clinical documentation. * Deliver an exceptional patient experience built on trust, education, and measurable progress. Rehabilitation \& Performance * Prescribe and progress exercise\-based rehabilitation programmes. * Support patients through the rehabilitation process using measurable goals and outcomes. * Utilise objective testing and clinical outcome measures where appropriate. * Assist in the delivery of return\-to\-running, return\-to\-sport, and performance\-based rehabilitation. * Work collaboratively with rehabilitation coaches, athletic therapists, and senior clinicians to optimise patient outcomes. * Develop confidence in using strength testing, movement analysis, and performance\-based rehabilitation principles. * Help bridge the gap between injury recovery and long\-term physical performance. Professional Development * Participate actively in mentorship sessions, clinical reviews, and in\-service training. * Engage with current research and evidence\-based practice. * Contribute to case discussions and team learning activities. * Develop confidence in clinical reasoning, exercise prescription, and rehabilitation planning. * Work towards becoming an expert rehabilitation clinician within the Resync framework. * Take ownership of your personal and professional development. Team Contribution * Contribute positively to clinic culture and team development. * Support clinic standards around patient experience, documentation, and communication. * Collaborate effectively with colleagues across physiotherapy, rehabilitation, and performance services. * Take ownership of your own learning and professional growth. * Promote the Resync philosophy and standards of care in every patient interaction. Essential Criteria * CORU registered Physiotherapist. * Minimum of 2 years post\-graduate musculoskeletal clinical experience. * Strong interest in musculoskeletal rehabilitation and exercise\-based therapy. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Strong organisational and clinical record\-keeping abilities. * Commitment to evidence\-based practice and ongoing professional development. * Ability to work independently while seeking support when appropriate. * A passion for helping people achieve meaningful outcomes through rehabilitation and coaching. Desirable Criteria * Experience working with sporting populations. * Experience delivering rehabilitation in a gym environment. * Additional training in exercise prescription, sports rehabilitation, strength and conditioning, dry needling, or related areas. * Membership of the Irish Society of Chartered Physiotherapists (ISCP). * Interest in performance, return\-to\-sport, and objective testing. * Experience working with runners, field\-sport athletes, or post\-operative rehabilitation clients. Personal Attributes **We Are Looking For Someone Who** * Enjoys coaching and educating patients. * Is curious, motivated, and eager to develop. * Takes ownership of patient outcomes. * Values teamwork and collaboration. * Embraces feedback and continuous improvement. * Has a genuine passion for helping people return to the activities they enjoy. * Thinks beyond pain relief and understands the value of strength, movement quality, confidence, and performance. * Wants to build a long\-term career within a progressive rehabilitation environment. What We Offer * Competitive salary with performance\-related bonus opportunities. * Structured mentorship from Clinical Specialists and Senior Physiotherapists. * Weekly clinical mentoring and regular CPD opportunities. * Access to a purpose\-built rehabilitation gym and advanced testing technology. * Clear career progression pathway into Senior and Clinical Specialist roles. * Opportunities to work alongside physiotherapists, athletic therapists, rehabilitation coaches, and performance specialists. * Exposure to a diverse caseload including sporting injuries, post\-operative rehabilitation, running injuries, persistent pain, and return\-to\-performance cases. * A supportive, collaborative, and ambitious team environment. * The opportunity to develop expertise in rehabilitation, performance, and return\-to\-sport pathways. Our Mission At Resync Physiotherapy, our goal is not simply to reduce pain. Our mission is to apply elite\-level rehabilitation, performance, and coaching principles to both athletic and general populations, helping people move better, become stronger, regain confidence, and return to the activities that matter most to them. Whether a patient wants to complete a marathon, return to competitive sport, keep up with their children, or simply walk without pain, our approach remains the same: objective assessment, structured rehabilitation, measurable progress, and expert coaching every step of the way. We believe everyone deserves access to the same standard of care traditionally reserved for elite athletes. If you are passionate about rehabilitation, eager to continue developing your skills, and want to work in an environment that values learning, coaching, and clinical excellence, we'd love to hear from you. Career Level Senior Education Level Bsc Salary 55000 Apply Before 01 Sep 2026 Location Stepaside Dublin Country Ireland County Dublin Contact Name Ciara Contact Email heretohelp@resyncphysiotherapy.ie Contact Phone 016855181

title: Platform Engineer
description: **About Us:** At Sitecore, our mission is to simplify how brands reach, engage, and serve people by delivering intelligent, personalised digital experiences that connect the world. This role is hands\-on and focused on implementing and maintaining Azure\-based infrastructure, Kubernetes environments, Infrastructure as Code, and observability tooling. **What You’ll Do:** * Design, deploy, and maintain cloud infrastructure on Microsoft Azure (e.g., AKS, ACR, networking, storage, policies) following established architectural standards. * Support and operate Kubernetes clusters, including deployments, scaling, upgrades, and routine troubleshooting. * Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform, including reusable modules and environment\-based configurations. * Implement and improve observability using Prometheus (metrics and alerting) and Grafana (dashboards and basic alerting), with optional support for Loki where applicable. * Support Linux\-based environments (Ubuntu/RHEL), including troubleshooting, basic performance tuning, and security best practices. * Collaborate with developers and senior engineers to improve deployment workflows, platform reliability, and internal tooling. * Participate in on\-call rotations and assist in incident response and post\-incident follow\-ups. * Contribute to documentation, automation, and platform improvement initiatives. **What You Need to Succeed:** * 3–5 years of experience in platform, cloud, DevOps, or infrastructure engineering roles. * Solid understanding of Microsoft Azure fundamentals and core services. * Practical experience working with Kubernetes (deployments, Helm, services, basic networking and scaling concepts). * Experience using Terraform for infrastructure provisioning (modules and multi\-environment setups preferred). * Good Linux administration skills (processes, networking basics, logging, troubleshooting). * Familiarity with monitoring and observability tools such as Prometheus and Grafana. * Basic scripting ability in Bash or Python. * Understanding of version control (Git) and CI/CD concepts. * Strong willingness to learn, collaborate, and improve engineering practices. **Additional Skills That Could Set You Apart:** * Exposure to AWS services (e.g., EC2, EKS, IAM). * Familiarity with observability tools such as Loki, OpenTelemetry, or ELK stack. * Exposure to GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux) or policy\-as\-code concepts (OPA/Gatekeeper). * Interest in infrastructure automation and platform engineering best practices. **What We Offer** * Opportunity to own critical platform components end\-to\-end * Modern toolset and high\-impact work (Azure \+ K8s \+ observability focus) * Collaborative, high\-performing team environment **Equal Opportunities at Sitecore** Sitecore is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity without unlawful regard to race, colour, ancestry, religion, gender, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, veteran status or any other local legally protected characteristic. **\#HYBRID**

with quality assurance and quality control (QA/QC) processes and maintaining associated records. * Supporting procurement and commercial teams with document administration and information management. * Attending site meetings, carrying out site inspections, and assisting with general project coordination activities. * Ensuring all activities are completed in accordance with company procedures and ISO standards (9001, 14001, and 45001\). **Qualifications \& Experience** * Previous experience in document control, project administration, construction support, or a similar role is desirable but not essential. * A qualification in Construction Management, Building Services, Engineering Technology, Business Administration, or a related discipline would be advantageous. * Strong organisational skills with excellent attention to detail. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel, Word, and Outlook. * Experience with document management systems (Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, SharePoint, Asite, etc.) would be beneficial. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and work effectively in a fast\-paced project environment. * A proactive attitude and willingness to learn. **Why Join QDG?** At Quinn Downes Group, we believe in empowering our people and providing the right support to help you reach your potential. * Competitive remuneration package * Career development through CPD programmes and tailored training * Exposure to diverse sectors and high\-profile projects * Discounted gym membership \& Employee title: Project Support Coordinator
description: Our Client is hiring a Project Coordination Specialists to provide project management and reporting support to the Project Manager to ensure the execution of major infrastructural projects on time and within budget. Working under the guidance of Project Managers and the PMO, the role ensures project activities progress according to schedule, budget, and governance standards, contributing to the company’s low\-carbon and renewable energy goals. **Responsibilities:** * Work with the PMO project team and support the successful delivery of large\-scale infrastructure projects. Assist in the development of project management plans in the areas of scope, schedule, cost, quality, resource, communications, risk, procurement and stakeholder management. Ensure project governance is adhered to and assist with project reporting to senior management and external stakeholders * .Build good working relationship with the project team to ensure that procedures and processes are understood and followed by all. Assist in knowledge transfer across the PMO team and wider Business * .Coordinate project communications including the preparation of meetings, reports and presentations. Administer the document management system and co\-ordinate the transfer of project information across a diverse and multi\-geographical project team. Utilise the online document control software to distribute and monitor documents for approval and ensure satisfactory revision control is maintained * .Monitor and control project activities, gather and summarise the outputs of project work streams to ensure that deliverables are recorded and acted upon; including attending project meetings to record and follow up on agreed actions. Prepare project specific control documentation using available templates, including project programmes, financial tracking, risk registers, issues log and action log * .Partake in public engagement with the project team, ensuring a proactive approach to community engagement in line with company policy is pursued on projects under management. Liaise with management and stakeholders to keep them appraised of project status * .Support in the procurement of all goods and services required by the project in accordance with company policy and legislative requirements. Prepare purchase order requisitions, co\-ordinate the review of service contracts and monitor project spend. Generate monthly financial report including comparison to approved budget estimate and forecast costs to completion * .All employees are expected to co\-operate fully with all provisions taken by the company for ensuring Health and Safety, and Welfare of co\-employees and members of the public using the premises * .Proactively participate in the Performance Management process to ensure delivery of own, and the wider team's objectives **. What we're looking fo** * r:Third\-level degree in Engineering, Business, Project Management or a related discipline (Master’s degree advantageous). Post graduate qualification in project management desirable. Minimum 1 years’ experience in co\-ordination role on large scale energy or infrastructure project deliver * y.Competency across a broad range project management skills must be demonstrated. Familiarity with energy infrastructure sector in Ireland desirabl * e.Excellent technical appraisal, report writing and communications skills essential. Professional project management certification (e.g. PMP or equivalent) is desirabl * e.Proficient in MS Office and MS Project. Good knowledge and proficiency in online sharing platforms such as SharePoint, Share file, ProjectWise etc. is a distinct advantag e.

title: Property Management Surveyor
description: **JLL empowers you to shape a brighter way**. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology for our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people and empowering them to thrive, grow meaningful careers and to find a place where they belong. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades or technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, join our team as we help shape a brighter way forward. Job Description**Surveyor \- Property Management** **Grade P2** ------------ **About JLL** ------------- **JLL shapes the future of real estate through world\-class services, advisory, and technology. The Surveyor role in Property Management (PM) team focuses on delivering exceptional property management, contributing to the Centre of Excellence, and maintaining strong client relationships.** -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- **The Role** This role is a high profile Retail Management Role working a mixed portfolio, including a trophy shopping centre (Cabot Circus) for 2 key JLL clients, Hammerson and L\&G. **Office location** The role is based in Bristol and will sit within JLL’s Property Delivery Team. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ ### **Client \& Stakeholder Support** * Support the Property lead as a key contact for the Asset Manager across allocated assets. * Maintain professional and responsive communication with clients, occupiers, and stakeholders. * Assist in preparing client reports and attending meetings where required. ### **Lease and Legal Management** * Assist in receiving, progressing, and preparing recommendations on occupier applications (licence to alter, wayleaves, underletting, assignments). * Monitor tenant compliance with lease covenants and obligations, escalating issues and supporting resolution. * Respond to routine technical queries in relation to leases and licences, referring more complex matters where appropriate. * Support resolution of occupier matters that may influence asset performance or valuation. ### **Financial Management** * Support financial processes including raising charges, monitoring income, and liaising with accounts teams. * Assist with arrears management in collaboration with credit control teams. * Maintain accurate financial records in line with internal processes and client requirements. ### **Service Charge** * Assist in the preparation and management of service charge budgets, reconciliations, and cash flow forecasts. * Ensure compliance with RICS guidelines and support achievement of client KPIs. ### **ESG** * Support the delivery of ESG initiatives in collaboration with FM and specialist teams. * Assist in implementing client ESG strategies across the portfolio. ### **Supplier \& Facilities Management** * Support management of third\-party suppliers, monitoring performance and escalating issues where necessary. * Work collaboratively with colleagues to ensure service delivery meets required standards. ### **Inspections** * Undertake regular property inspections, ensuring assets are well presented and issues are identified, recorded, and progressed for resolution. ### **Transactions / Transitions** * Support property transitions, including onboarding and offboarding assets and assisting with due diligence processes. * Liaise with solicitors and third parties under the direction of the Associate. ### **Occupier Engagement \& Customer Experience** * Develop positive relationships with occupiers and support initiatives to enhance customer satisfaction. * Gather occupier intelligence and feedback for reporting to the client. * Assist in the delivery of customer experience initiatives across the portfolio. ### **Works** * Support delivery of major works and site\-based projects, working with consultants, contractors, and internal teams. ### **Health \& Safety** * Maintain an understanding of relevant Health \& Safety legislation and support compliance across properties. ### **Insurance** * Assist with the insurance renewal process and day\-to\-day insurance matters. * Support administration of insurance claims through to resolution. ### **Void Property Management** * Support management of vacant units, ensuring compliance with client and insurer requirements. ### **Fees** * Assist in capturing and tracking fee\-earning opportunities in line with agreed processes. **Additional Responsibilities** ------------------------------- * Support business development activities and identify opportunities to enhance client relationships. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the team. * Assist with project\-based work (internal and external). * Identify and help resolve legacy property management issues. **Candidate Profile** --------------------- * Ideally working towards MRICS or recently qualified. * Solid experience in property management or related role. * Good understanding of landlord and tenant matters. * Strong organisational skills with attention to detail. * Commercially aware with a proactive approach to problem solving. * Strong communication skills and ability to build relationships. * Competent IT skills, including property management systems. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. **Behavioural Skills** ---------------------- * Collaborative team player supporting colleagues and wider team objectives. * Professional and confident communicator (internal and external). * Willingness to learn and develop technical expertise. * Positive, proactive, and solution\-oriented mindset. * Adheres to JLL working practices and values. * Contributes to an inclusive and supportive team culture. **Location:** On\-site –Dublin, IRL If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! At JLL, we harness the power of artificial intelligence (AI) to efficiently accelerate meaningful connections between candidates and opportunities. Using AI capabilities, we analyze your application for relevant skills, experiences, and qualifications to generate valuable insights about how your unique profile aligns with the specific requirements of the role you're pursuing. ***JLL Privacy Notice*** Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL), together with its subsidiaries and affiliates, is a leading global provider of real estate and investment management services. We take our responsibility to protect the personal information provided to us seriously. Generally the personal information we collect from you are for the purposes of processing in connection with JLL’s recruitment process. We endeavour to keep your personal information secure with appropriate level of security and keep for as long as we need it for legitimate business or legal reasons. We will then delete it safely and securely. For more information about how JLL processes your personal data, please view our Candidate Privacy Statement. For additional details please see our career site pages for each country. Jones Lang LaSalle (“JLL”) is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the employment process – including the online application and/or overall selection process – you may email us at HRSCLeaves@jll.com. This email is only to request an accommodation. Please direct any other general recruiting inquiries to our Contact Us page \> I want to work for JLL.

title: Quality Control Microbiology Analyst
description: Orion Group Life Sciences are currently recruiting a **Quality Control Microbiology Analyst (Shift)** on behalf of our Multinational Biopharmaceutical Client based in Dundalk on an initial 11\-Month contract with potential to extend. Shift: 20% shift premium 2 days one week and 5 days next week * Support laboratory operations within the new laboratory facility on a shift basis (primarily extended days), timing subject to change depending on business needs. * Support laboratory operations within the new laboratory facility which will include support of equipment qualification (PQ), documentation generation of SOP’s, Work instructions as well as contributing to lab and process establishment. * Reporting and review of laboratory data, to support generation of batch data, to collate and analyse end of quarter and year reports for all raw data generated. * Maintaining and release of laboratory stock, ordering laboratory consumables where required. * Manage and ensure routine cleans of laboratory equipment are completed as per site procedures, ensure laboratory housekeeping standards are maintained, identifying continuous improvement opportunities in laboratory processes. * Active participation in functional meetings, facilitation of cross\-functional meetings where required. * Participation in laboratory investigations and Root Cause Analysis activities. * Participation in internal and external audit and inspection activities. * Participation in SME development groups to improve technical expertise. * Perform supporting activities for general lab operations, execute tasks such as EM, method verifications, and execution of laboratory studies were required. * A good knowledge of GMP, familiarity with USP, EP, JP requirements. * Ability to solve problems and take new perspectives on existing solutions with a desire to continuously learn, improve and develop. * Excellent troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills as an individual contributor as well as collaboration skills and teamworking ability to work with global and interdisciplinary teams. * Build team effectiveness skills, learn to promote team success through own actions and by demonstrating respect and appreciation for diverse populations. * Support timely training completion and coaching of team members. **What skills you will need:** * A degree qualification in a relevant field with preference for background in Microbiology. * A minimum of 1\-3 years experience working in a relevant academic or industry field, (Internships and student placements will be considered in lieu of experience). * Ability to work as part of a team and on own initiative in a constructive manner. * Familiarity with microbiological testing techniques. * Knowledge of laboratory systems (e.g. LIMS, E\-logs, and electronic notebooks). * Ability to think logically and be proactive under pressure. * Effective communicator and technical expertise in science, to be able to share and communicate that knowledge through training other analysts. **People** are our business **worldwide** Orion Group was founded in 1987 and is now one of the largest, independent, international recruitment companies. We have a network of 200 employees working from 24 offices, delivering a range of services – Talent Acquisition, Recruitment Outsourcing Services, Retained Search, Global Workforce Solutions, Completions \& Commissioning and Materials Management – across 68 countries. As a global leader in workforce solutions, we recruit personnel across the Engineering \& Technical, Office \& Commercial, Scientific and Skilled Trades disciplines, for sectors including Oil \& Gas, Life Science, Power \& Utilities, Constructions \& Infrastructure, Manufacturing and Renewables.

title: Quantity Surveyor - Commercial/Residential Projects - Dublin
description: **Your new company** You will be joining a well‑established and highly regarded construction contractor with a strong track record of delivering complex, high‑value projects across Ireland. The organisation operates across a broad range of sectors including residential, commercial, education, healthcare, industrial and mixed‑use developments.With a reputation for excellence in quality, safety, and project delivery, the company is recognised for its professionalism, structured approach, and commitment to building long‑term relationships with clients and stakeholders. The business continues to grow through repeat work and new project wins, creating an environment where talented commercial professionals can develop their careers on high‑profile projects.Working as part of a collaborative and experienced team, you will have the opportunity to play a key role in the successful delivery of projects, contributing directly to both financial performance and operational excellence. **Your new role** As a Quantity Surveyor, you will take responsibility for managing the commercial aspects of construction projects from early stages through to completion and final account. You will work closely with project managers, engineers, and subcontractors to ensure strong cost control, efficient procurement, and effective contract administration throughout the project lifecycle.Key responsibilities will include: * Managing project costs from tender stage through to final account settlement. * Preparing and reviewing budgets, cost plans, and financial forecasts. * Assisting in tendering processes, including analysis of subcontractor and supplier quotations. * Procuring subcontract packages, negotiating terms, and managing subcontractor agreements. * Managing subcontractor accounts, including interim payments, valuations, and final accounts. * Preparing and submitting interim valuations, variations, claims, and cost reports. * Monitoring cost performance and reporting on project financial status through regular reporting mechanisms. * Producing cost value reconciliations (CVRs) and tracking project profitability. * Identifying commercial risks and implementing mitigation strategies to protect project margins. * Attending project meetings with clients, consultants, and subcontractors to provide commercial input. * Ensuring compliance with contractual obligations and industry standards. * Supporting the wider commercial team and contributing to continuous process improvement. **What you'll need to succeed** * A degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related discipline. * Relevant experience working as a Quantity Surveyor or Assistant QS within a construction environment. * Strong understanding of construction processes, procurement routes, and cost control principles. * Knowledge of construction contracts and commercial management practices. * Excellent analytical and numerical skills with a strong attention to detail. * Good negotiation and communication skills with the ability to build relationships with stakeholders. * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team on multiple project elements. * Strong organisational and time management skills with the ability to meet deadlines. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and commercial/reporting software. * A proactive mindset and willingness to learn and develop within a fast‑paced environment. **What you'll get in return** * The opportunity to work on large, high‑profile construction projects across multiple sectors. * A structured and supportive working environment with strong leadership and mentorship. * Competitive salary and benefits package aligned with your experience. * Clear career progression opportunities within a growing and successful contractor. * Exposure to all stages of the construction lifecycle, enhancing your commercial expertise. * Ongoing professional development and training opportunities. * A collaborative culture that encourages growth, innovation, and continuous improvement. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. Hays Ireland is a trading division of Hays Specialist Recruitment (Ireland) Limited and acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and employment business for the supply of temporary workers. By applying for this job you accept the T\&C's, Privacy Policy and Disclaimers which can be found at hays.co.uk

description: * **Limitless earning potential:** With monthly uncapped commissions, you’re in control and can create financial security for yourself and those that matter to you. * **Rapid financial progression:** Achieve in two years what others take a decade to reach * **Continuous Training:** Training goes beyond onboarding, helping you become an industry\-leading expert At Access Healthcare, recruitment isn't just a job; it's a fast track to real financial growth. Every relationship you build, and every placement you make, moves you closer to your new house, your new car, the new life you are seeking. No more salary caps and no more yearly raises. **What We Offer** * **Uncapped commission and rapid earning potential** directly linked to your performance (Year 1 expectations on average for **Basic salary \+ Unlimited commission is €45K\+;** Year 2: **€75K\+** ) * **Financial growth is directly aligned with your performance:** More deals lead to higher earnings, faster progression, and a stronger sense of impact and recognition * **Merit\-based advancement** \- promotions based purely on results, not time served. * **Fast\-track clear career progression** with the opportunity to earn multiple promotions within 12 months **What we’re looking for** * At least 1 year of post\-graduation experience, ideally in sales or client\-facing roles * Confident communicator with strong relationship\-building skills * Ability to commute to Dublin 2, Monday to Friday * Ambitious, self\-motivated, and driven to exceed expectations * EU Passport or Stamp 4 Visa required For more information, contact me at daniel.moreira@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+ (353\) 1 572 1201 to learn more about this thrilling opportunity.

title: Reinstatement Agent - GNI
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC are currently looking for an experienced **Reinstatement Agent** to join our team. The Reinstatement Site Agent is responsible for managing and coordinating reinstatement activities associated with Gas Networks Ireland (GNI) infrastructure works, including excavation reinstatement, permanent road and footpath restoration, and ensuring full compliance with GNI specifications and local authority requirements. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Oversee day\-to\-day reinstatement works across multiple GNI project sites, ensuring they are completed safely, on time, and to the required standards. Coordinate reinstatement crews, subcontractors, traffic management teams, and materials suppliers. Conduct site inspections to verify compliance with GNI specifications, permits, method statements, and local authority conditions. Enforce HSEQ policies in line with GNI requirements. Coordinate with local authorities regarding permits, road opening licences (ROL), traffic management plans, and inspections. Support planning of reinstatement operations, including resource allocation, material ordering, and sequencing of works. Demonstrate strong commercial awareness by managing costs effectively, identifying opportunities for efficiency, and ensuring value for money in all reinstatement activities. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Proven experience in reinstatement, utilities, civil engineering on gas infrastructure projects. Strong understanding of road reinstatement, compaction processes, materials standards, and traffic management. Knowledge of GNI specifications, road opening licensing (MapRoad), and utility reinstatement standards. Excellent organisational and site coordination skills. Strong communication and stakeholder\-management abilities. Full, clean driving licence. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Remote Work: Market Research Telephone Interviewers. Evening/Weekends
description: We are looking for fully remote staff for our telephone interviewing team. We are looking for long\-term staff for evening and/or weekend shifts. Those who are showing a good performance may be asked to continue on to day\-time shifts but this is project dependent. This is a great work opportunity for anyone looking for experience in a market research/communications environment, for a flexible work commitment and a pleasant working atmosphere. **Flexible hours available.** **Competitive rates**. Conducting Market Research Surveys over the phone with members of the general public on a diverse range of subjects. **Requirements:** A quiet location to work where you will not be disturbed for the duration of your shift Windows Laptop compatible with Windows 10 or above An ethernet cable to plug directly in to a broadband router A headset with a microphone **No selling involved** and no experience required. You need a good telephone voice, professional and polite telephone manner, must have a confident outlook. You need basic keyboard skills. Full training will be given to successful applicants. You will choose your hours from shifts between 5pm \- 9pm Monday to Friday and/or 10am \- 2pm Saturdays. Shifts from 2pm \- 9pm are also an option subject to project availability. Shifts on Sundays are subject to project availability and are from 11am\-3pm. Preferred candidates would be able to commit to at least 3 shifts a week \- **You choose when you work**. Please note hours are subject to availability and are not guaranteed. Your starting Rate of Pay will be €14\.15 per hour however, depending on your performance and length of service with us (a minimum of 3 months) there is an excellent opportunity for you to qualify for the higher rates. Rates of Pay: Level 0 \- €14\.15 per hour Level 1 \- €14\.75 per hour Level 2 \- €15\.75 per hour Level 3 \- €16\.75 per hour Holiday and Bank Holiday benefit is paid monthly at the rate of 14\.5% **PLEASE NOTE:** Successful applicants are not eligible to take holiday leave for the first three months of employment (unless arranged with management prior to commencement) Please include at least one reference with your application. All applicants please apply using the form below only, do not contact us via telephone or direct e\-mails. Job Type: Part\-time Pay: €14\.15\-€16\.75 per hour Benefits: * Employee assistance program * Work from home Application question(s): * Do you have a quiet, private work space? * What is your current address? * Do you have your own windows laptop? Experience: * Call center: 1 year (preferred) Language: * English (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dublin 6, CO. Dublin

title: Retail Artist
description: Retail Artist \- Brown Thomas Blanchardstown***About Charlotte Tilbury Beauty****Founded by British makeup artist and beauty entrepreneur Charlotte Tilbury MBE in 2013, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has revolutionised the face of the global beauty industry by de\-coding makeup applications for everyone, everywhere, with an easy\-to\-use, easy\-to\-choose, easy\-to\-gift range. Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty continues to break records across countries, channels, and categories and to scale at pace.* *Over the last 10 years, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has experienced exceptional growth and is one of the most talked about brands in the beauty industry and beyond. It has become a global sensation across 50 markets (and growing), with over 2,300 employees globally who are part of the Dream Team making the magic happen.* *Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty is a truly global business, delivering market\-leading growth, innovative retail and product launches fuelled by industry\-leading tech — all with an internal culture of embracing challenges, disruptive thinking, winning together, and sharing the magic. The energy behind the brand is infectious, and as we grow, we are always looking for extraordinary talent who want to be part of this our success and help drive our limitless ambitions.* *About the Role** You can work at a fast pace to achieve and exceed KPI’s. You feed on the buzz of achieving your goals and being the best, you can be. You do not want to meet expectation – you exceed it. * “Team work makes the dream work” – is your working motto. You understand your impact and how vital your role is. You can adapt to change at the last minute and be a real support to your managers and colleagues alike. * You champion the art of world\-class artistry and an impeccable customer experience. You are a Charlotte Tilbury brand ambassador and want to pass on your knowledge to customer after customer. You live and breathe the Charlotte Tilbury makeup looks – and can recreate the magic of these on your customer, as well as offering advice and guidance. You pride yourself on knowing your customers are returning back to you. * You follow the company grooming standards to a tee – you know what good looks like and are not afraid to display this. *About You** You will ideally have had some experience as a Sales Advisor. Ideally this will be gained from a fast\-paced environment such as beauty, retail or FMCG. * You aspire to be part of a beauty brand that disrupts the way it is seen – and continues to grow. You love all things Charlotte Tilbury – and are not afraid to spread the word. * Ideally you will have experience in applying makeup – and knowing how to adapt your style to different customers with different needs. * You champion positivity – and can think in a limitless way that makes your energy shine. *Why join us?** You can only be the best when you are set up to succeed – not only do we offer on the job training. We offer you the opportunity to attend Charlotte’s Magic Academy – where dreams are truly made. Combining art, commerce and Charlotte’s own values. You will feel truly empowered to live the magic in your role. * Opportunities to disrupt processes (in a positive way!) and have your voice truly heard. * The opportunity to be part of a company where we celebrate our achievements, and you are included. With a real scope for growth and progression to support your own development. * You receive exclusive launches of our incredible products before anyone else – not only that you even receive a full Charlotte Tilbury look once you pass your probation. (That is right all the products from one of our iconic looks) * We also offer an incredible staff discount, and access to Tilbury Treats – our very own rewards platform allowing you to save money and gain money cannot buy discounts on anything from gym memberships to cinema tickets. *At Charlotte Tilbury Beauty, our mission is to empower everybody in the world to be the most beautiful version of themselves. We celebrate and support this by encouraging and hiring people with diverse backgrounds, cultures, voices, beliefs, and perspectives into our growing global workforce. By doing so, we better serve our communities, customers, employees \- and the candidates that take part in our recruitment process.* *If you want to learn more about life at Charlotte Tilbury Beauty please follow our* *LinkedIn page!*
